title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.
notes: Implemented a9a7ab1: wait_for_backend() retries backend::select() for up to 120s, sleeping 5s between attempts. serve subcommand uses this instead of choose_backend().

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.
notes: Implemented f202638: lima-vm@colima.service patched to Type=forking, drop --foreground, RemainAfterExit=yes, TimeoutStartSec=300, Restart=no, ExecStop=. Deployed + checked in to docs/lima-vm-colima.service.

title: Design periodic GitHub Actions stuck-run reaper
notes: Design target: add a periodic, dry-run-first stuck-run reaper for jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai and other QUEUE_REPO values. It must be job-aware: do not classify a run as stale only by workflow_run.created_at because reruns/current attempts can have old run.created_at but new job.started_at/run_started_at. Proposed policy: every 10-15 minutes, scan queued and in_progress runs with REST; fetch jobs for candidates; compute current_attempt_age from max(job.started_at, run.run_started_at, run.updated_at) for queued jobs. Actions: (1) cancel-then-delete only queued run objects whose run.created_at > 8h AND current_attempt_age > 60-90m, (2) cancel superseded queued runs only when a newer queued or in_progress run exists for same repo/workflow/branch and candidate current_attempt_age > 20-30m, (3) never touch in_progress by default; report in_progress jobs over policy separately with runner/job URL, (4) label-mismatch detector reports queued jobs whose labels have no matching online runner labels, e.g. agent-orchestrator or warp-*; it does not cancel them. Install path: systemd user timer on Linux host using ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha or scripts/reap-stuck-runs.sh, with log file under ~/.local/state/ezgha/reaper.jsonl and alert() on mutations/failures. Safety: dry-run default, --apply only for timer after config enables it, max mutations per run, REST rate-limit floor, lock file, exact URLs in logs, no tail cancellation without explicit allowlist.

title: Pace runner respawns to avoid tripping host watchdog(8) reboot
description: ensure_count/docker_backend currently can respawn many runner slots simultaneously (JIT + docker run per slot), which spikes 1-min load average enough to trip /etc/watchdog.conf's max-load-1=24 threshold on this 32-thread box -- confirmed root cause of two host reboots today (00:48 loadavg 71 on a full cold respawn of 16; 12:50 loadavg 25-28), the second of which killed the runner-truly-healthy mission's predecessor sidekick session outright. Operator/session-level discipline (checking uptime before every systemctl restart) is not durable -- it dies with the session. Fix: add bounded concurrency to ensure_count (e.g. max 3-4 simultaneous docker run + JIT registration calls in flight, batched with a short sleep between batches) and/or a load-aware backoff (check /proc/loadavg or getloadavg() before spawning a batch, delay if already elevated) in docker_backend.rs, so the daemon is safe to restart by ANY operator at ANY time regardless of how many slots need respawning at once. This also directly serves the E1/INV-1 utilization invariant: gentler respawn waves mean shallower busy-count dips, which should show up as fewer inv1_fail_class=offline-respawning samples in invariant_history.jsonl once this lands -- that data itself is a validation signal for the fix. Coordinate scope with the parallel Jeff-Ubuntu crash-investigation sidekick's staggered-respawn ask (goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/04-coordination-crash-mission.md) to avoid duplicating work. Also feeds E4's ed8 fleet-churn root-cause requirement (docs/ed8-fleet-churn-root-cause-20260707.md).

title: Cap E1 invariant sampler job-enumeration cost + share fetch with queue_monitor
description: The E1 daemon-native invariant sampler (src/queue_monitor.rs, InvariantSamplerState/sample_invariants) currently calls fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet per monitored repo, which loops over EVERY queued run and calls gh api list_workflow_jobs once per run to get job-level data. With queued_jobs=1290 measured live, a single tick can take minutes of sequential API calls (observed: alert fired 263s after the sample's own ts), which likely contributes to the GitHub secondary rate limit already affecting Gate 4. Approved fix (main's design, 2026-07-07 14:xx PT), scope: (a) cap per-tick job enumeration to the OLDEST ~50 queued runs per repo -- INV-2 stays exact since the oldest job necessarily lives among the oldest runs (sort by created_at ascending, take first 50 before the per-run job fetch loop); add a queued_jobs_capped:bool field to the InvariantSample schema, true whenever a repo had more than 50 queued runs at fetch time, so the JSONL honestly signals queued_jobs is a lower bound rather than an exact count in that case. (b) share ONE fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet result per repo per daemon tick between QueueMonitorState::maybe_check and InvariantSamplerState::maybe_sample instead of each independently re-fetching the same worldarchitect.ai data -- likely requires restructuring main.rs's serve loop to fetch once and pass the snapshot(s) to both tick handlers, or introducing a small per-tick snapshot cache keyed by repo with a short TTL. Update/add unit tests for the cap logic (e.g. 60 queued jobs input -> only oldest 50 considered, queued_jobs_capped=true; 30 input -> all considered, queued_jobs_capped=false) and for the shared-fetch behavior if a cache is introduced. Coordinate with bead ez-gh-actions-po2 (respawn pacing) -- both target the same underlying GitHub API rate-limit pressure from different angles (this bead reduces sampler-induced API load; po2 reduces the docker-run/JIT-registration burst that trips the host watchdog) and both should be dispatched to codex together once quota returns (~14:52 PT 2026-07-07).

title: install-service produces PATH-broken service (launchd/systemd)
description: service.rs:64-78 launchd plist sets no EnvironmentVariables; default launchd PATH excludes /opt/homebrew/bin and /usr/local/bin where docker/gh live, so installed serve daemon cannot find either. launchctl load -w also bails if already loaded (re-run breaks). Linux: After=docker.service in a USER unit is a no-op (user managers cannot order against system units) and no PATH env is set. Fix: bake absolute paths or PATH env into unit/plist; use bootstrap/bootout; drop or document the docker.service ordering.

title: fix(ezgha.service): Restart=always + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600
description: Base unit currently has 'Restart=on-failure' + 'RestartSec=10'. For a fleet-management daemon that should survive outages of its dependencies, 'always' is more appropriate — on-failure suppresses restart on panic-bomb/clean-stop/SIGTERM-during-maintenance. With RestartSec=15 + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600, the 6th restart happens ~10min after the 1st (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 600s-cap), well past any lima cold-boot. systemd 255 (verified installed) supports all three knobs. Edit ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service base file generated by src/service.rs.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: StartLimitIntervalSec=300 + StartLimitBurst=5 + RestartSec=30 added to service unit generation in service.rs. Restart=on-failure kept (Restart=always would fight with lima dep).

title: Async IO-wait offload: Deploy PR Preview should not hold self-hosted runner during cloud build/deploy
description: Provenance: 2026-07-07 observed 4 concurrent Deploy PR Preview jobs holding self-hosted runners 15-21m each while babysitting Cloud Run build/deploy (mostly gcloud API waits, not compute). Patterns (proven in-repo by #8244 Gate-4 split + Gates 7/8 precedent): (1) split poll/wait phases to ubuntu-latest job; (2) evaluate moving deploy ENTIRELY to ubuntu-latest if pure API calls (check secrets/WIF requirements); (3) true async: trigger deploy + exit, completion webhook -> repository_dispatch -> verdict workflow (zero runner-held wait). Also audit mcp-smoke preview job for same pattern. Do AFTER queue stabilizes; one PR per workflow.

title: systemd WatchdogSec=60 kills serve during ensure_count batch (Linux flapping root cause)
description: Provenance: journalctl jeff-ubuntu 2026-07-07 — ezgha.service: Watchdog timeout (limit 1min)! every ~90s; SIGABRT; restart counter climbing. src/main.rs:320-332 pings WATCHDOG=1 only AFTER ensure_count returns; batch spawn of 4+ runners (JIT gh + docker run) routinely exceeds 60s. Deploying install-service (jleechan-1kl) turned stable 16/16 into 13-16 flapping. Fix: ping watchdog at loop start AND/OR WatchdogSec>=180 AND/OR ping during long ensure_count. Immediate ops: disable or widen WatchdogSec on Linux unit until code fix.

title: fix(docker): format cpus argument to 2 decimal places to prevent float precision error
description: Docker run fails with exit code 125 when cpus is a high-precision float (e.g. 0.8571428571428571). Formatting it to 2 decimal places resolved this.

title: BUG: free_disk_gb guard measures Colima VM overlay, not host FS (docker_backend.rs:761-780)
description: Swarm adversarial review 2026-07-07 (coldstart, confirmed against real code). ezgha's disk-floor guard free_disk_gb runs 'docker run --entrypoint df' (src/docker_backend.rs:761-780) which measures the Docker/Colima VM overlay filesystem, NOT the macOS/Linux HOST filesystem. On Colima the VM overlay and host are different volumes. Impact TODAY (independent of cold-start): the disk-floor protection can read healthy while the host disk (where ~/.config/ezgha, images, logs, and any future mirror cache live) is critically low, or vice-versa — the guard may be protecting the wrong filesystem. Fix: add/replace with a host-side statvfs check on the actual paths ezgha writes; document which filesystem each guard covers. This is a prerequisite for the cold-start mirror (jleechan-yov P3 BLOCKER A) but stands alone as a current-code defect.

title: queue-reaper-stopgap: harden against gh API rate-limit exhaustion (dual-bucket + backoff + call-budget)
description: Provenance: ~/Library/Logs/ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap.log ticks 02:41:54Z + 02:48:18Z 2026-07-08 — both failed 'gh: API rate limit exceeded' (HTTP 403, core bucket drained by concurrent pollers: reaper per-run status polls from the force-cancel fallback, sidekick monitor loop, jeff-ubuntu doctor, main session checks — all one token). Result: no sweep for 30+min, 45 runs crossed 20m (queue regression 62->87). Fixes for scripts/cleanup-stuck-runs.sh + queue-reaper-stopgap.sh: (1) check gh api rate_limit (quota-exempt) FIRST, skip tick gracefully if core remaining < ~200 with a log line, (2) REST->GraphQL fallback per the dual-bucket rule (separate 5000/hr buckets), (3) reduce per-run polls: batch the post-cancel status check into one list call instead of N per-run gets, (4) cross-session budget: reaper is the PRIORITY consumer — monitor loops should sample less often when reaper is active. Also consider Rust-native reaper (qbl) inheriting the same protections.
close_reason: Implemented + live-verified 2026-07-07 20:5x PT, commit 071ce2e. (1) Listing 403s: backoff 60s, one retry, then graceful tick-skip exit 0 — pre-check via rate_limit is IMPOSSIBLE for the secondary throttle (core read ~3900 remaining while /actions/runs 403'd), so 403 handling is the gate; (2) per-cancel status polls removed — batched force-cancel verification via ONE re-listing (new stat tail_force_cancelled); (3) zombie delete path keeps per-run poll. Live apply run: 3 cancelled, 1 force-cancelled survivor, 0 failures. Deployed via Gate 0 (cargo install + kickstart). GraphQL fallback listed in the bead was NOT implemented — graceful-skip makes it non-critical; open a follow-up if throttle windows prove long enough to matter.

title: GitHub-side wedge: in_progress job survives cancel+force-cancel (200 OK) after runner VM death
description: Provenance: Mac Colima resize 2026-07-07 ~19:3x PT (sidekick STATE.md /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/runner-queue-healthy-mac/STATE.md). Run 28912311508 (deploy-preview) started on ez-mac-runner-b-3 in the snapshot-to-stop race window; after VM restart the old runner registration (id 137026) stayed wedged on GitHub: cancel AND force-cancel both returned 200 but job still shows in_progress; runner DELETE returns 422 'currently running a job'. Not fixable via API — GitHub-side heartbeat timeout must expire. Implications: (1) reaper design (ez-gh-actions-qbl) should time-bound this case and defer rather than retry-loop; (2) any VM interrupt protocol must expect a snapshot-then-act race and a possibly-wedged registration afterward; ezgha's own retry loop clears it once GitHub's side times out. Operational lesson also applies to the pending Linux VM resize.

title: Optimize daily-campaign-report: replace auth.list_users + per-user subcollection scan with collection_group + count aggregation
description: Provenance: Lane D codex investigation 2026-07-07 (bead rev-a10h9), 97.48min outlier breakdown: 70m53s infra (setup-python 32m42s + pip 38m11s → addressed by cold-start Phase 1 wheelhouse) + 24m49s GENUINE script time. Root cause of script time: unbounded N-users × per-campaign-story-stream query — auth.list_users() then per-user subcollection scan, streaming every user/campaign just to compute counts. No retry loops. Fix: (1) collection_group('campaigns') query + Firestore count() aggregation instead of streaming; (2) bound duplicate-scan to top-N post-filter candidates; (3) add per-phase timing so the 120min timeout (PR #8247) distinguishes infra vs script time. worldai repo change (mvp_site report script), separate PR, needs measurement. Natural re-validation case for cold-start Phase 1.
